pred_reason: The chosen labels SWC-101 (Integer Overflow and Underflow) and SWC-107 (Reentrancy) are reasonable because the contract contains both multiplication overflow risks in totalSupply() and reentrancy vulnerabilities in the Release function. Evidence: Release function makes external calls before state changes (reentrancy risk); totalSupply() multiplies WETH balance by 1 billion without overflow protection; Dangerous state patterns align with SWC-101 and SWC-107 classifications

findings.Description: The Release function makes an external call before completing state changes, creating a reentrancy risk. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential loss of tokens if malicious ERC20 token reenters the contract

findings.Issue: Unsafe external calls in IBOT interactions
findings.Description: External calls to IBOT.ShowConfiguration() lack validation of returned addresses. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential contract takeover if malicious IBOT implementation is used

findings.Issue: Dangerous state change pattern after external calls
findings.Description: The Release function makes external calls before state changes (though no state changes currently follow). This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential reentrancy attacks if state changes are added later

findings.Description: totalSupply() multiplies WETH balance by 1 billion without overflow protection. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Incorrect token supply reporting if WETH balance is large

findings.Description: Contract lacks proper privilege separation and initialization safeguards. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Complete contract takeover if privileged accounts are compromised

findings.Description: Release function doesn't check return value from ERC20 transfers. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Silent failures could occur during token transfers

findings.Description: EncryptedSwap function has inconsistent balance checking logic. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential artificial token inflation if used incorrectly

findings.Description: transferFrom uses uint(-1) for unlimited approvals without safeguards. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential arithmetic issues and unexpected behavior

findings.Description: EncryptedSwap lacks validation for zero amount/value inputs. This issue appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Potential spamming and fake balance manipulation
